mechanism_narrative: Monthly payroll growth captured by PAYEMS generates a persistent wealth effect and aggregate demand impulse that drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days as investors anticipate corporate revenue expansion.
failure_condition: Rolling 2-year Rank IC falls below 0.03 or positive return hit rate in PAYEMS expansion months is statistically indistinguishable from the unconditional 65.5% baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, restrictive monetary policy significantly elevates discount rates and corporate borrowing costs, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as liquidity contracts.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the median return when FEDFUNDS > 4.5% is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in industrial production and rising unemployment creates a joint supply-labor slack signal that amplifies recession pricing, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days beyond either variable alone.
failure_condition: The interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05) beyond individual INDPRO and UNRATE effects, or 20-day hit rate of negative returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, sharp declines in retail sales trigger immediate negative repricing of cyclical earnings expectations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as algorithmic and discretionary funds reduce exposure.
failure_condition: RSAFS declines coincide with positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations during identified late-cycle periods, or the mean 5-day return is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment combined with elevated federal funds rates creates a double-drag on household income and credit availability, jointly forcing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as recession probability spikes.
regime_hint: restrictive policy with labor market deterioration
failure_condition: The UNRATE-FEDFUNDS interaction fails to achieve a t-stat > 2.0 in predicting 60-day returns, or the joint signal underperforms the unconditional negative drift.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production falls by more than 1% over a rolling three-month period, it signals acute supply-side contraction that reduces corporate operating leverage and margins, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of cases, or the median 20-day return is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month percent increase in consumer prices exceeding 0.3% forces rapid repricing of near-term real interest rates, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as front-end yields adjust.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL acceleration months produce negative 5-day returns in fewer than 55% of instances, or the median 5-day return is positive.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above 2.5% annualized signals robust aggregate demand that supports corporate revenue beats, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as growth optimism persists.
failure_condition: When GDPC1 exceeds 2.5%, subsequent 60-day positive return rate is below 60%, or the median return is not positive.
